summary: Large fighter CPU attack decomp/matching PR for src/melee/ft/ftcpuattack.c. It replaced placeholder declarations for ftCo_800B4AB0, ftCo_800B52AC, ftCo_800B5AB0, and ftCo_800B77E8 with C implementations, refined ftCo_AttackEntry fields and temporary target-physics structs, updated ftCo_800B5AB0's header signature, changed Fighter_x1A88_t.x50 from int to u32, and made smaller matching-oriented edits in ftCo_800B8A9C and ftCo_800BB220. The only loaded feedback is an automated progress report showing seven unmatched-item improvements; there are no loaded human review comments.

key_files.role: Main decomp/matching file; adds implementations for CPU attack-selection functions and supporting local structs, includes math_ppc.h, and adjusts nearby matching-sensitive functions.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: ftCo_800B8A9C call to ftCo_800B5AB0 gains cpu->x50 argument

key_files.role: Prototype surface updated for recovered ftCo_800B5AB0 signature.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: ftCo_800B5AB0 changed from int ftCo_800B5AB0(Fighter*, void*) to int ftCo_800B5AB0(Fighter*, void*, void*)

key_files.role: Fighter CPU state type correction for x50.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: Fighter_x1A88_t.x50 changed from int to u32

what_changed.text: Implemented ftCo_800B4AB0, ftCo_800B52AC, and ftCo_800B5AB0 as attack-list filtering/selection routines that iterate ftCo_AttackEntry arrays, reject commands already in cpu->xCC_array, check CPU level and timing gates, predict relative positions using cur_pos/pos_delta/gravity/terminal velocity, and select a weighted command using HSD_Randf.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: bot report shows ftCo_800B4AB0 0.00% -> 91.73%, ftCo_800B52AC 0.00% -> 91.12%, ftCo_800B5AB0 0.00% -> 93.60%

what_changed.text: Expanded ftCo_AttackEntry from mostly padding to explicit fields cmd, x04, x08, x0C, x10, x14, weight, x1C, and x20; added temporary ftCo_x50_attr and ftCo_x50_t structs to express accesses in ftCo_800B5AB0.

Implemented ftCo_800B77E8 as a fighter-kind switch that appends selected command IDs into cpu->xCC_array based on target visibility/collision checks, facing direction, ground/air state, and some character-specific conditions.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: replaces /// #ftCo_800B77E8 with void ftCo_800B77E8(Fighter* fp)
diff.diff: switch(fp->kind) cases for Mario, Fox/Falco, Captain/Ganon, Pikachu/Pichu, Koopa/GKoops, Yoshi, Luigi, Peach, Kirby

what_changed.text: Adjusted ftCo_800B8A9C so the ftCo_800B5AB0 call passes cpu->x50 as the new second argument before the attack-table argument.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: ftCo_800B8A9C hunk changes result = ftCo_800B5AB0(fp, (void*) cpu->x50, ((void**) Fighter_804D64FC->x14)[fp->kind])

what_changed.text: Made smaller matching-oriented edits in ftCo_800BB220: initialized temp_r31 at declaration, rewrote item-kind tests as a switch, and introduced dx/dy/dz temporaries before assigning projected hit positions.
Updated public declarations/types to match recovered use: ftCo_800B5AB0 has three parameters in ftcpuattack.h and Fighter_x1A88_t.x50 is u32 in types.h.

smart_moves.text: The PR propagated the recovered ftCo_800B5AB0 signature to the owning header and updated the callsite instead of hiding the extra argument behind a local declaration.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: ftcpuattack.h signature changed to int ftCo_800B5AB0(Fighter*, void*, void*)
diff.diff: ftCo_800B8A9C passes cpu->x50 to ftCo_800B5AB0

smart_moves.text: The main selection routines use typed Fighter fields such as co_attrs, cur_pos, pos_delta, facing_dir, ground_or_air, x34_scale, and x1A88 members rather than raw pointer arithmetic for known Fighter state.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: ftCo_800B4AB0/52AC/5AB0 access fp->co_attrs, target->co_attrs, fp->cur_pos, target->cur_pos, fp->pos_delta, target->pos_delta, fp->x1A88

smart_moves.text: For ftCo_800BB220, introducing dx/dy/dz temporaries narrows repeated vector-delta expressions and may have been a matching tactic while also improving readability.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: ftCo_800BB220 repeated hunks replace inline (hit->x4C - hit->x58) expressions with f32 dx/dy/dz

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a recovered body proves a function takes an additional context pointer, update the owning header and all visible callsites together; this PR changed ftCo_800B5AB0 from two to three parameters and passed cpu->x50 at the callsite.

For large matching functions over table data, first recover the table stride into a small local struct with offset-labeled fields; ftCo_AttackEntry was changed from padding around cmd/weight to explicit fields used throughout the new selection functions.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: list->x04, list->x08, list->x0C, list->x10, list->x14, list->x1C, list->x20 accesses in ftCo_800B4AB0/52AC/5AB0

decomp_lessons.lesson: Small temporary structs can be useful bridge types when a pointee layout is partly understood but not globally named; ftCo_x50_t and ftCo_x50_attr express the x50 target/object fields used by ftCo_800B5AB0 without raw casts at every access.

naming_conventions.text: Unproven data/layout fields remain offset-style names such as x04, x08, x0C, x10, x14, x1C, x20, x50, xC0, and xCC, while clearer local roles use names like fp, target, cpu, list, sel, count, found, nearzero, relx, fpPredY, relPredY, rangeF, and rangeB.

Some decompiler-style temporaries remain in the large ftCo_800B77E8 implementation, such as temp_r30 and var_r0; the loaded evidence does not show whether these were reviewed or intended as final names.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: ftCo_800B77E8 declares Fighter* temp_r30 and int var_r0

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Added math_ppc.h alongside math.h, likely to support matching math code that calls sqrtf and/or PPC math forms in the new prediction logic.
Preserved several explicit f64 casts and unsuffixed double constants such as 0.5, 1.0, 5.0, and 1000.0 in matching-sensitive arithmetic rather than normalizing every literal to f32.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: ftCo_800B4AB0/52AC/5AB0 prediction expressions use f64 casts and 0.5

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Used explicit near-zero checks as paired comparisons against 0.00001f and -0.00001f, then a bool nearzero, instead of a macro or fabs form.
Used a switch over item kind in ftCo_800BB220 for a two-case special item filter, replacing an if expression over It_Kind_Unk4 and It_Kind_Star.

Introduced declaration-order and local-lifetime changes in ftCo_800BB220, including initializing temp_r31 at declaration and inserting block-local dx/dy/dz variables for repeated coordinate projections.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: Automated GALE01 report lists seven improvements in unmatched items: ftCo_800B77E8 to 88.33%, ftCo_800B4AB0 to 91.73%, ftCo_800B52AC to 91.12%, ftCo_800B5AB0 to 93.60%, ftCo_800BB220 to 93.90%, .sdata2 to 91.88%, and ftCo_800B8A9C to 87.29%.
No loaded review comments or human review records are present, so reviewer intent and requested changes cannot be inferred.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: only PR body and decomp-dev[bot] issue comment loaded

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching ftCo_800B4AB0 or ftCo_800B52AC to see whether the duplicated prediction logic was factored or renamed.
Search for later changes to ftCo_x50_t, ftCo_x50_attr, or Fighter_x1A88_t.x50 to determine whether the temporary layout received a canonical type/name.
Search ftcpuattack.c history for temp_r30, var_r0, p1/L1/f1/n1 naming cleanup after PR 2526.
Search later PRs for ftCo_800B77E8 to see whether its repeated character-case collision checks were looped, macroed, or left as matching-required unrolled code.
Search for PRs referencing ftCo_800BB220 dx dy dz or switch(ip->kind) to identify whether those were stable matching tactics.

changed_files.jsonl: three changed files with line counts
diff.diff: concrete hunks for ftcpuattack.c, ftcpuattack.h, types.h
text_corpus.jsonl: empty PR body and one automated decomp-dev[bot] report
notes: The diff excerpt and loaded raw diff are truncated in the prompt, but targeted local reads covered the header/type hunks and key smaller ftCo_800B8A9C/ftCo_800BB220 changes.
There is no PR body explaining author intent.
There are no loaded human review comments, so no human feedback or rationale is available.
The automated report gives percentage improvements, not exact match claims; this intake does not claim any function fully matched.
ftCo_800B77E8 contains repeated per-character blocks and temporary-style names; without review or later evidence, that shape should not be promoted as a general style lesson.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Propagate recovered ftCo_800B5AB0 signature through header and callsite
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2526 recovered ftCo_800B5AB0 as taking three arguments, updated src/melee/ft/ftcpuattack.h accordingly, and changed ftCo_800B8A9C to pass cpu->x50 as the new context argument before the attack table.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use offset-labeled bridge structs for partially recovered attack data
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2526 expanded ftCo_AttackEntry and introduced ftCo_x50_t/ftCo_x50_attr as local bridge structs so the new CPU attack-selection code could use structured field accesses while preserving unproven offset-style names.

source_update_candidates.title: Do not overgeneralize repeated per-character blocks from partially matched CPU AI functions
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2526's ftCo_800B77E8 implementation uses repeated per-character local blocks and temporary-style names while the bot report shows partial progress, not a full match. This may be matching-required for this PR, but should not become a general style preference without later verifier/review evidence.
evidence_refs: diff.diff: ftCo_800B77E8 declares p1/L1/f1/n1 through p10/L10/f10/n10 and temp_r30/var_r0

source_update_candidates.reason: Candidate caution for curator review only; aligns with existing natural-loop and conservative-naming standards, but current PR evidence is not enough to add a new global rule.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the unrolled p1/L1/f1/n1 through p10/L10/f10/n10 pattern in ftCo_800B77E8 as accepted source style; the loaded evidence lacks verifier detail and the automated report shows only partial improvement.

Do not claim full matches for ftCo_800B4AB0, ftCo_800B52AC, ftCo_800B5AB0, ftCo_800B77E8, ftCo_800B8A9C, or ftCo_800BB220; the only loaded validation is a bot percentage report with non-100% after values.
Do not infer reviewer approval rationale beyond the PR being merged; no human review comments are loaded.
Do not promote semantic names for ftCo_AttackEntry fields or ftCo_x50_t fields; the PR keeps offset-style names, indicating semantics are not proven in the loaded evidence.
